name: Ecwid
one_liner: Ecwid (by Lightspeed) is a hosted ecommerce platform that lets merchants add an online store to any website or social channel, with a REST API for managing products, orders, customers and store settings.
access_notes: Developers create a free custom app in the Ecwid control panel and copy a public (read-only) token and secret (full-access) token from the app's Details page. For single-store use the secret token can be used directly as a Bearer token with no OAuth flow. Docs recommend setting up a separate free test store since there is no dedicated API test mode. Public multi-store apps use the OAuth 2.0 install flow. Working credentials are obtainable with no payment or human approval.
api_notes: JSON REST API over HTTPS covering products (and variations/combinations), categories, orders, customers, discounts/coupons, general store settings/profile, and more. Rate-limited to ~600 requests/minute per token (429 on exceed). Full CRUD across many resource families puts operation count near/above 100.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server found from Ecwid/Lightspeed. Multiple third-party MCP options exist (Zapier, Pipedream, viaSocket, Vinkius, Apify scraper) that wrap the Ecwid API, but none are official.
main_blocker: None significant; single-store secret token is trivially obtained but public multi-store distribution requires the OAuth app-registration/install flow.

evidence.claim: Ecwid uses OAuth 2.0 with private (write) and public (read-only) access tokens for REST API authentication.

Custom apps provide public and secret tokens copied from the app Details page; secret token can be used directly as Bearer token; docs recommend a separate free test store since there is no API test mode.
REST API covers orders, products, customers, categories, discounts and store settings with ~600 requests/minute per token rate limit.
Ecwid supports webhooks (e.g. order.created, product.updated) validated via X-Ecwid-Webhook-Signature sha256 header.

open_questions: Exact total operation count not enumerated; whether a paid Ecwid plan tier is required for certain API scopes (e.g. some endpoints may be gated by store subscription level).
